name: Szechuan Scallops
recipeIngredient: 3 Sweet bell peppers, red, green, yellow or 1 onion cut into 1-inch squares
1 large Sweet onion; halved, cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
2 tablespoons Dry sherry/other white wine
2 tablespoons Light soy sauce
1/2 teaspoon Ground ginger
1 teaspoon Hot red pepper flakes -- or to taste, hot red peppers
2 cups Fresh or thawed scallops (about 10 ounces)
3/4 cup Spicy tomato juice
1. Arrange all ingredients near the stove. Prepare a large nonstick skillet or electric frying pan, spraying liberally with cooking spray.
2. Arrange onion in the skillet in a single layer and cook over medium heat until it begins to brown.
3. Combine all ingredients except scallops in the skillet; cover, bring to a simmer, and cook for 5 minutes.
4. Uncover and continue to simmer until the sauce thickens (about 4-5 more minutes). Stir in the scallops and cook just until heated through and coated with the sauce.
5. Serve with plain rice if desired. Makes 4 servings, approximately 175 calories each; Rice adds about 110 calories per half-cup.
